children.name: Monticola
children.description: Monticola — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Monticola angolensis
children.description: The Miombo rock thrush is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is found in Angola, Botswana, Burundi, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Malawi, Mozambique, Rwanda, Tanzania, Zambia, and Zimbabwe. Its natural habitat is dry savanna.

children.name: Monticola brevipes
children.description: The short-toed rock thrush is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is found in Angola, Botswana, Namibia, and South Africa. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical dry shrubland.

children.name: Monticola cinclorhynchus
children.description: The blue-capped rock thrush is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Monticola explorator
children.description: The sentinel rock thrush is a species of passerine bird in the Old World flycatcher family Muscicapidae that is found in Lesotho, South Africa, and Eswatini. Its natural habitat is rocky outcrops in high-altitude grassland.

children.name: Monticola gularis
children.description: The white-throated rock thrush is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae of the order Passeriformes.

children.name: Monticola rufiventris
children.description: The chestnut-bellied rock thrush is a species of passerine bird in the Old World flycatcher family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Monticola rufocinereus
children.description: The little rock thrush is a species of passerine bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is found in Eritrea, Ethiopia, Kenya, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Somalia, South Sudan, Tanzania, Uganda, and Yemen. It is found in rocky areas with some trees, and sometimes near settlements.

children.name: Monticola rupestris
children.description: The Cape Rock-Thrush is a member of the bird family Muscicapidae. This rock-thrush breeds in eastern and southern South Africa, Lesotho and Eswatini. It is a common endemic resident, non-migratory apart from seasonal altitudinal movements in some areas.

children.name: Monticola saxatilis
children.description: The common rock thrush, also known as the rufous-tailed rock thrush or simply rock thrush, is a species of chat that breeds in southern Europe across Central Asia to northern China. This species is strongly migratory, all populations wintering in Africa south of the Sahara. It is an uncommon visitor to northern Europe.

children.name: Monticola solitarius
children.description: The blue rock thrush is a species of chat. This thrush-like Old World flycatcher was formerly placed in the family Turdidae. It breeds in southern Europe, northwest Africa, and from Central Asia to northern China and Malaysia.

children.name: Cyanoptila
children.description: Cyanoptila — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Stiphrornis
children.description: Stiphrornis — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Cyornis cyanopolia

children.description: #REDIRECT Blue-fronted blue flycatcher Category:Taxa named by Adolf Bernhard Meyer.

children.name: Cyornis infuscata
children.description: Cyornis infuscata — a species in the genus Cyornis, family Muscicapidae. Also known as old world flycatchers.

children.name: Sigelus
children.description: Sigelus — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Sheppardia
children.description: Sheppardia — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Sheppardia aurantiithorax
children.description: The Rubeho akalat is a member of the Old World flycatcher family, (Muscicapiidae), known from the Rubeho Mountains in Dodoma Region. The mountains are a part of the Eastern Arc of Tanzania. Akalats trapped in 1989 here were assumed to be an isolated population of Iringa akalat which occurs c.

children.name: Sheppardia bocagei
children.description: Bocage's akalat is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is found in Angola, Cameroon, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Equatorial Guinea, Nigeria, Tanzania, and Zambia. Its natural habitats are boreal forests, subtropical or tropical dry forests, subtropical or tropical swamps, and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

children.name: Sheppardia cyornithopsis
children.description: The lowland akalat is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. Its disjunct range extends across the African tropical rainforest. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forest and subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest.

children.name: Sheppardia gabela
children.description: The Gabela akalat is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is endemic to Angola. The name is in part derived from the town where they were first observed, Gabela.

children.name: Sheppardia gunningi
children.description: The east coast akalat or Gunning's robin is a small passerine bird which can be found in the east of Africa from Kenya to Mozambique, and is named after J.

children.name: Sheppardia lowei
children.description: The Iringa akalat is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is endemic to Iringa Region of Tanzania. The species is named after the collector Willoughby Prescott Lowe.

children.name: Sheppardia montana
children.description: The Usambara akalat, also known as the Usambara alethe or Usambara robin-chat, is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is endemic to the Usambara Mountains in Tanga Region of Tanzania.

children.name: Sheppardia sharpei
children.description: Sharpe's akalat is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is found in Tanzania and northern parts of Zambia and Malawi. Its natural habitats are boreal forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

children.name: Myioparus
children.description: Myioparus — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Myioparus griseigularis
children.description: The grey-throated tit-flycatcher is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is widespread across the African tropical rainforest.

children.name: Myioparus plumbeus
children.description: The grey tit-flycatcher is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It has an extensive but patchy distribution in sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Erithacus
children.description: Erithacus — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Erithacus hachisukae
children.description: Erithacus hachisukae — a species in the genus Erithacus, family Muscicapidae. Also known as old world flycatchers.

children.name: Erithacus rubecula
children.description: The European robin, known simply as the robin or robin redbreast in the British Isles, is a small insectivorous passerine bird belonging to the Old World flycatcher family Muscicapidae. It is found across Europe, as far east as Western Siberia, and as far south as North Africa. It is sedentary in the west and south of its range, and migratory in the north and east of its range where winters are harsher.

children.name: Pinarornis
children.description: Pinarornis — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Cercotrichas
children.description: Cercotrichas — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Chaimarrornis

children.name: Tarsiger
children.description: Tarsiger — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Xenocopsychus
children.description: Xenocopsychus — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Saxicola
children.description: Saxicola — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Saxicola merula
children.description: Saxicola merula — a species in the genus Saxicola, family Muscicapidae. Also known as old world flycatchers.

children.name: Saxicola rubetra
children.description: The whinchat is a small migratory passerine bird breeding in Europe and western Asia and wintering in central Africa. At one time considered to be in the thrush family, Turdidae, it is now placed in the Old World flycatcher family, Muscicapidae. Both sexes have a strong supercilium, brownish upper parts mottled darker, a pale throat and breast, a pale buff to whitish belly, and a blackish tail with white bases to the outer tail feathers, but in the breeding season, the male has an orange-buff throat and breast.

children.name: Saxicola rubicola
children.description: The European stonechat is a small passerine bird that was formerly classed as a subspecies of the common stonechat. Long considered a member of the thrush family, Turdidae, genetic evidence has placed it and its relatives in the Old World flycatcher family, Muscicapidae. It is found across Europe, as far east as Ukraine and the South Caucasus, and in parts of North Africa.

children.name: Saxicola spec
children.description: Saxicola spec — a species in the genus Saxicola, family Muscicapidae. Also known as old world flycatchers.

children.name: Saxicola syenitica
children.description: Saxicola syenitica — a species in the genus Saxicola, family Muscicapidae. Also known as old world flycatchers.

children.name: Saxicola thoracica
children.description: Saxicola thoracica — a species in the genus Saxicola, family Muscicapidae. Also known as old world flycatchers.

children.name: Namibornis
children.description: Namibornis — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Luscinia
children.description: Luscinia — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Luscinia aedon
children.description: Luscinia aedon — a species in the genus Luscinia, family Muscicapidae. Also known as old world flycatchers.

children.name: Luscinia luscinia
children.description: The thrush nightingale, also known as the sprosser, is a small passerine bird in the Old World flycatcher family. It is a migratory species breeding in forests in Europe and the Palearctic and overwintering in Africa. An insectivore, it nests near the ground in dense undergrowth.

children.name: Luscinia megarhynchos
children.description: The common nightingale, rufous nightingale or simply nightingale, is a small passerine bird which is best known for its powerful and beautiful song. An Old World flycatcher, it belongs to a group of more terrestrial species, often called chats. Its range partly overlaps with that of the more northerly thrush nightingale, a closely related species with which hybrids have occurred.

children.name: Luscinia svecica
children.description: The bluethroat is a small passerine bird in the Old World flycatcher family. It is a migratory insectivorous species breeding in wet birch wood or bushy swamp in Europe and across the Palearctic with a foothold in western Alaska. It nests in tussocks or low in dense bushes.

children.name: Muscicapa striata
children.description: The spotted flycatcher is a small passerine bird in the Old World flycatcher family. It breeds in most of Europe and in the Palearctic to Siberia, and is migratory, wintering in Africa and south western Asia. It is declining in parts of its range.

children.name: Muscicapa tessacourbe
children.description: Muscicapa tessacourbe — a species in the genus Muscicapa, family Muscicapidae. Also known as old world flycatchers.

children.name: Ficedula elisae
children.description: The green-backed flycatcher is a bird in the family Muscicapidae, which contains the Old World flycatchers. It was long considered to be a subspecies of the narcissus flycatcher, but morphological and acoustical differences between the two indicate they are instead separate species. It breeds in northeastern China and winters in southeast Asia.

children.name: Ficedula erithacus
children.description: The slaty-backed flycatcher is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Ficedula hodgsoni
children.description: The pygmy flycatcher, also known as the pygmy blue-flycatcher, is a bird species of the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Ficedula hypoleuca x albicollis
children.description: Ficedula hypoleuca x albicollis — a species in the genus Ficedula, family Muscicapidae. Also known as old world flycatchers.

children.name: Ficedula riedeli
children.description: The Tanimbar flycatcher is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is found on the Tanimbar Islands. Its habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

children.name: Ficedula sordida
children.description: The slaty-backed flycatcher is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Enicurus
children.description: Enicurus — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Enicurus coronatus
children.description: Enicurus coronatus — a species in the genus Enicurus, family Muscicapidae. Also known as old world flycatchers.

children.name: Enicurus maculatus
children.description: The spotted forktail is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is found in the Himalayas and the hills of Northeast India, Bangladesh, Myanmar, and southern China including Yunnan. Birds of this species are 25 cm with a long tail.

children.name: Enicurus schistaceus
children.description: The slaty-backed forktail is a species of forktail in the family Muscicapidae. A slim, medium-sized forktail, it is distinguished from similar species by its slate grey forehead, crown, and mantle. It has a long and deeply forked tail banded in black and white, a white rump, and a white bar across its primary feathers; the rest of the plumage is predominantly white.

children.name: Enicurus scouleri
children.description: The little forktail is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. The specific name commemorates Dr. John Scouler of Glasgow.

children.name: Pogonocichla
children.description: Pogonocichla — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Pogonocichla margaritata

children.description: Phoenicurus — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Phoenicurus ochruros
children.description: The black redstart is a small passerine bird in the Old World flycatcher family. It is a widespread breeder in rocky habitats across Eurasia and North Africa, from the British Isles south to Morocco and east to central China. It is resident in the milder parts of its range, but northern and eastern birds migrate.

children.name: Phoenicurus ochruros x phoenicurus
children.description: Phoenicurus ochruros x phoenicurus — a species in the genus Phoenicurus, family Muscicapidae. Also known as old world flycatchers.

children.name: Humblotia

children.description: Swynnertonia — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Oenanthe oenanthe
children.description: The northern wheatear or wheatear is a small passerine bird in the Old World flycatcher family. It is the most widespread member of the wheatear genus Oenanthe in Europe and North and Central Asia. The northern wheatear is a migratory insectivorous species breeding in open stony country in Europe and east across the Palearctic with footholds in northeastern Canada and Greenland as well as in northwestern Canada and Alaska.

children.name: Cinclidium
children.description: Cinclidium — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Myophonus

children.name: Myiomela

children.name: Leonardina
children.description: Leonardina — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Leonardina woodi
children.description: The Bagobo babbler or Bagobo robin is a monotypic species of bird with its taxonomy undergoing numerous changes and is currently classified as Muscicapidae or an old world flycatcher. It is endemic to the Philippines, only found on Mindanao. It was once deemed "unquestionably the Philippines most secretive bird" by ornithologist Robert Kennedy.

children.name: Campicoloides
children.description: Campicoloides — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Campicoloides bifasciatus
children.description: The buff-streaked chat or buff-streaked bushchat is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is found in Lesotho, South Africa, and Eswatini. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical dry lowland grassland.

children.name: Pinarochroa
children.description: Pinarochroa — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Pinarochroa sordida
children.description: The moorland chat, also known as the alpine chat or hill chat, is a species of songbird in the Old World flycatcher family. It is endemic to the eastern Afromontane. It lives at high altitudes on moors and grassland, usually above 3,400 m (11,100 ft), but can live as low as 2,100 m (6,900 ft).

children.name: Hodgsonius
children.description: Hodgsonius — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Hodgsonius phoenicuroides
children.description: The white-bellied redstart is a species of bird of the family Muscicapidae. It is found in Bhutan, China, India, Laos, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, Thailand, and Vietnam, where its natural habitat is temperate forests.

children.name: Pentholaea
children.description: Pentholaea — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Pentholaea albifrons
children.description: The white-fronted black chat is a species of passerine bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is native to the Sudan (region). Its natural habitats are moist savanna and subtropical or tropical dry shrubland.

children.name: Pentholaea arnotti
children.description: Arnot's chat, also known as the white-headed black-chat, is a species of bird in the chat and flycatcher family Muscicapidae. The species is found in southern Africa from Rwanda and Angola to South Africa.

children.name: Pentholaea collaris
children.description: Arnot's chat, also known as the white-headed black-chat, is a species of bird in the chat and flycatcher family Muscicapidae. The species is found in southern Africa from Rwanda and Angola to South Africa.

children.name: Larvivora
children.description: Larvivora — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Larvivora sibilans
children.description: The  rufous-tailed robin  is a small passerine bird. Its breeding range extends from southern Siberia and the Sea of Okhotsk to southern China and southeastern Asia.

children.name: Emarginata
children.description: Emarginata — a genus of old world flycatchers in the family Muscicapidae.

children.name: Emarginata schlegelii
children.description: The Karoo chat is a small passerine bird of the Old World flycatcher family Muscicapidae. It is a common resident breeder in southwesternmost Angola, western Namibia and western South Africa. Its habitat is Karoo and desert scrub in the south, extending to the escarpment zone in the north.

children.name: Emarginata tractrac
children.description: The tractrac chat is a small passerine bird of the Old World flycatcher family Muscicapidae. It is a common resident breeder in southernmost Angola, western Namibia and western South Africa.

children.name: Emarginata trilucida
